title: Unlocking the chemical potential of filamentous fungi using prime editing - Nature Biotechnology
gist: Researchers built fPE7max, a prime editor optimized for filamentous fungi enabling precise edits from single bases to multi-kilobase insertions/deletions, and used it to edit translation-regulatory elements to unlock previously inaccessible fungal biosynthetic pathways and novel metabolites.

insights.claim: A prime editor named fPE7max, optimized for filamentous fungi, enables precise genome engineering across diverse species—spanning single-base substitutions, kilobase-scale insertions, and multi-kilobase deletions.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: A broad-range editing platform for fungi expands the addressable design space for microbial natural-product and enzyme production.
insights.quote: from single-base substitutions to kilobase-scale insertions and multi-kilobase deletions, across diverse species
insights.locator: We developed the prime editor fPE7max

insights.claim: Editing translation-regulatory elements (upstream open reading frames) for a pleiotropic metabolic regulator activated previously inaccessible biosynthetic pathways and uncovered new fungal metabolites.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: Translational-control editing is a route to mining silent fungal pathways—relevant to drug-discovery and specialty-chemical pipelines.
insights.quote: unlocked previously inaccessible biosynthetic pathways and uncovered new fungal metabolites

insights.claim: Editing upstream open reading frames to control gene translation, first pioneered in plants, is here applied as a lever for metabolic activation in fungi rather than relying on coding-sequence knockouts.
insights.speaker: Nature Biotechnology
insights.so_what: uORF editing is a transferable strategy for tuning gene expression across organisms without altering protein sequence.
